chunks.page_summary: This page provides a technical overview of components and basic connection instructions for integrating IO-Link devices, specifically Rheonics sensors, with an S7-1200 system using an IO-Link Master SM 1278.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

IO-Link integration with S7-1200 with IO-Link Master SM 1278
Modified on: Tue, 23 Apr, 2024 at 7:35 PM

1. Components
1.1. IO-Link Master
An IO-Link master serves as a crucial element in industrial automation, facilitating the connection and management of communication between sensors or actuators equipped with IO-Link technology and a higher-level control system, such as a PLC or PC.

1.2. IO-Link Adapter
The IO-Link adapter plays a pivotal role in industrial settings by enabling the seamless conversion between various communication protocols, ensuring smooth interoperability between different devices.

1.3. Rheonics SME
The SME-DRM is an ultra-fast and robust electronics combined with comprehensive computational models, suitable for mounting on DIN rails and inside cabinets. Powered by 24Volts, the Rheonics SME-DRM is equipped with an Ethernet port, SD card slot, Modbus RTU RS-485 port, and 3 channels for 4-20 mA Analog outputs.

1.4. SM 1278
The SM 1278 module enables data exchange with up to 4 external IO-Link devices via one 3-wire cable each or four standard actuators/standard sensors. Comprehensive parameterization options make it possible to adapt the controller flexibly to the communication partner. Thanks to the compatibility of IO-Link with standard sensors, commercially available sensors by IEC 61131 Type 1 can also be operated on the IO-Link master.

2. Hardware connection and block diagram
Connect the IO-Link adapter from the Rheonics sensor to an IO-Link Master device.

Wiring Diagram:
key_facts: The document describes the integration of IO-Link technology with an S7-1200 system using an IO-Link Master SM 1278.
An IO-Link master is essential for industrial automation, managing communication between IO-Link sensors/actuators and higher-level control systems like PLCs or PCs.
The IO-Link adapter facilitates seamless protocol conversion, ensuring interoperability between devices in industrial settings.

The Rheonics SME-DRM is an ultra-fast, robust electronic module designed for DIN rail mounting, equipped with an Ethernet port, SD card slot, Modbus RTU RS-485 port, and 3 channels for 4-20 mA Analog outputs.
The SM 1278 module supports data exchange with up to four external IO-Link devices using 3-wire cables and offers comprehensive parameterization for flexible controller adaptation.
The SM 1278 module is compatible with standard sensors, including those compliant with IEC 61131 Type 1, allowing commercially available sensors to be operated on the IO-Link master.
The last modification to this document was on Tuesday, April 23, 2024, at 7:35 PM.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides a procedural guide for connecting a Rheonics SME-DRM sensor via an IO-Link adapter to a Siemens SM 1278 IO-Link Master and subsequently integrating the IO-Link Master device within a TIA Portal S7-1200 project.
chunks.extracted_text:
Figure 1. IO-Link Master SM 1278 with Rheonics sensor SME-DRM
Rheonics IO-Link adapter
Pin 1: L+
Pin 3: M
Pin 4: IO-Link
Modbus TCP Ethernet RJ45
Reset
SD-Card
Applies to all SME Versions

1. Add the IO-Link master SM 1278 from the Hardware catalog in TIA PORTAL

Figure 2. Adding IO-Link Master device SM 1278 to S7-1200 project
IO-LINK Master
No 'properties' available.
No properties can be shown at the moment. There is either no object selected or the selected object does not have any displayable properties.

2. This way a 4-port IO-Link device is added to the project, next we need to open the SIMATIC S7 PTC Tool.
key_facts: The Rheonics IO-Link adapter connector utilizes Pin 1 for L+, Pin 3 for M, and Pin 4 for IO-Link communication.
The SME sensor module features Modbus TCP Ethernet RJ45 connectivity, a Reset function, and an SD-Card slot.
The Siemens SM 1278 IO-Link Master module is a 4-port device.
The first step in configuration involves adding the IO-Link master SM 1278 from the Hardware catalog in TIA PORTAL to an S7-1200 project.
After adding the IO-Link device, the next step is to open the SIMATIC S7 PTC Tool for further configuration.

4. Download the IODD file for the IO-Link converter which can be found here

key_facts: The S7-PTC software is used to configure IO-Link Master devices and IO-Link converters within the Siemens TIA Portal environment.
To initiate S7-PTC configuration for a device, right-click the device in the project tree and select 'Start device tool...' from the context menu.
A dialog box will appear to 'Select tool or access point', where 'S7-PCT' should be chosen, and the 'Start' button clicked.
IODD files for the IO-Link converter must be downloaded from the specified Banner Engineering website.
The downloaded IODD file needs to be imported into S7-PCT to complete the configuration of the IO-Link converter.
diagram_labels: Provides the configuration for IO-Link devices.

key_facts: IODD files for IO-Link devices are installed via the 'Options' menu -> 'Import IODD...' in the SIMATIC S7-PCT software.
The S15C-MGN-KQ device is an Inline converter that facilitates conversion from IO-Link to Modbus.
IO-Link converters are configured by dragging them from the 'IO-Link Port Configuration Tool' device tree onto a port of an IO-Link Master (e.g., SM 1278) in the main configuration area.
The SM 1278 IO-Link Master V2.1 has four configurable ports, with each port initially set to 'Deactivated' mode for Autosense.
The 'Addresses' tab in the SIMATIC S7-PCT software allows verification of PLC address assignments for IO-Link data.
The software provides an informational message if the selected object is neither an IO-Link master nor an IO-Link device.
diagram_labels: The selected object is neither IO-Link master nor IO-Link device.
Drag and drop the IO-Link converter

warnings_notes: The selected object is neither IO-Link master nor IO-Link device.

key_facts: The document demonstrates the configuration of an IO-Link Master in SIMATIC S7-PCT software for connecting an inline converter.
The configuration example uses a Banner Engineering S15C-MGN-KQ inline converter, which converts IO-Link to Modbus.
The S15C-MGN-KQ inline converter is specifically compatible with IO-Link revision 1.1.
Rheonics process data, including kinematic viscosity status, kinematic viscosity, and temperature median status, are mapped to specific PLC input word addresses.
The process involves defining the input and output data lengths for the IO-Link port and assigning corresponding PLC addresses for various process data parameters.
The user is instructed to click the 'show addresses' button to verify the assigned PLC addresses.
diagram_labels: Figure 7. IO-Link Master configuration in Ch1
Figure 8. IO-Link Master mapping in S7-PTC
warnings_notes: The device is only compatible with the IO-Link revision 1.1.

key_facts: The page describes the mapping of Rheonics sensor variables to a PLC.
Process data inputs, including temperature, density, and viscosity parameters, along with power supply and sensor status, are mapped to PLC registers.
Siemens TIA Portal software is used for configuring these PLC tags, specifically for a CPU 1212C DC/DC/DC.
The tags are explicitly added to facilitate IO-Link communication.
A final step in the process involves converting the mapped data to the correct format.
diagram_labels: Figure 9. Added tags for IO-Link communication

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates a PLC programming logic diagram detailing data conversion steps for Rheonics sensor readings and lists related technical references.
chunks.extracted_text:
Block interface

Network 8:
Comment:
MOVE block (EN, ENO)
  IN: %WR0 ("Parameter 12 Viscosity Last Good(0)")
  OUT1: #ARRAY[0]
MOVE block (EN, ENO)
  IN: %W32 ("Parameter 12 Viscosity Last Good(1)")
  OUT1: #ARRAY[1]

Network 9:
Comment:
SHL DWord block (EN, ENO)
  IN: #ARRAY[0]
  N: 16
  OUT: #tem_Word

Network 10:
Comment:
OR DWord block (EN, ENO)
  IN1: #ARRAY[1]
  IN2: #tem_Word
  OUT: %MD74 ("Parameter 12 Viscosity Last good IO-Link")
CONV DWord to Real block (EN, ENO)
  IN: %MD74 ("Parameter 12 Viscosity Last good IO-Link")
  OUT: %MD66 ("Parameter 12 Viscosity Last good IO-Link(1)")

Network 11:
Comment:

Figure 10. Data conversion for Rheonics sensor reading

3. References
1. SM 1278 4xIO-Link Master
   (https://mall.industry.siemens.com/mall/en/WW/Catalog/Products/10231178?activeTab=productinformation&regionUrl=WW)
2. https://www.bannerengineering.com/us/en/products/part.809836.html
   (https://www.bannerengineering.com/us/en/products/part.809836.html)
3. (https://support.industry.siemens.com/cs/document/32469496/s7-pct-v3-5-sp3-upd3-para-el-maestro-io-link?dti=0&lc=es-PE) https://support.industry.siemens.com/cs/document/32469496/s7-pct-v3-5-sp3-upd3-for-io-link-master?dti=0&lc=en-PE
   (https://support.industry.siemens.com/cs/document/32469496/s7-pct-v3-5-sp3-upd3-for-io-link-master?dti=0&lc=en-PE)
key_facts: The page illustrates a data conversion process designed for Rheonics sensor readings.
The conversion logic combines two parts of a viscosity reading, identified as 'Parameter 12 Viscosity Last Good(0)' and 'Parameter 12 Viscosity Last Good(1)', into a single DWord.

The process involves standard PLC operations: MOVE for data transfer, SHL (Shift Left) to position a 16-bit value, OR to combine two DWords, and CONV (Convert) to transform the DWord into a Real (floating-point) number.
The converted viscosity data is labeled as 'Parameter 12 Viscosity Last good IO-Link' and stored in memory addresses %MD74 and %MD66.
The document references a Siemens SM 1278 4xIO-Link Master, indicating the use of IO-Link communication for the sensor system.
Additional references point to Banner Engineering products and Siemens support documentation for S7-PCT V3.5 SP3 UPD3 for IO-Link Master.
diagram_labels: Figure 10. Data conversion for Rheonics sensor reading
